ypRft. Another doubt arises in mind as I count the limbs taken by

verse 62, as has been already dealt with. This kind of divi-
sion of the ground into 81 squares should be resorted to while plan-
ning the construction of cities, villages and houses.]

Or, by drawing nine lines across and nine vertically we can
get 04 squares. Here diagonals should be drawn from corner to
corner. In this diagram, Brahman rules over four squares at the

472 Bfhat Samhitd
centre; and the eight deities situated along the diagonals in the
corners near him rule over half a square i.e. Apah, Apavatsa,
Savitjr, Savitra, Indra, Jaya, Rajayaksman and Rudra; in the outer
corners, Sikhin, Antariksa, Anila, Mrga, Pitf, Papayaksman, Roga
and Diti, over half a square each; those that are on both sides of
these, over one square and a half i.e. Parjanya, Bhr£a, Pu^an,
